items.note: Washington and Tehran are still describing the same deal differently. US Vice-President JD Vance called the framework a major milestone and said inspector access could come within days, possibly the same day; Iran's foreign ministry said it made no new nuclear commitments and that cooperation runs through its own parliament and security council. Agreement in principle, no schedule for inspectors to reach the bombed Fordow, Natanz and Isfahan sites.

Hormuz is reopening on Tehran's terms. The IRGC rejected a new shipping route it was not consulted on, declared that only Iran-designated corridors are authorized, and told ships to keep contact with its navy; Iran waived planned fees for 60 days during talks while its negotiator said Hormuz will never return to its pre-war status. About 70 vessels crossed on Wednesday against a 120-to-140 pre-war norm, and Brent fell to $72.24, its lowest since before the war.

The warnings around El Obeid are getting louder as the bombardment intensifies. The RSF is massing around the North Kordofan capital and hitting it with drones; the US says mass atrocities could be imminent, the UN puts roughly 500,000 civilians at risk and counts about 50 killed by drones over ten days, and officials keep invoking El Fasher. A full ground assault inside the city is not confirmed, so the prevention window is still open and the protective action still lags the warning.

items.what: From the Beaufort Castle summit, Netanyahu said the army would hold the southern security zone “as long as necessary” and “we are not going to withdraw,” undercutting the US-backed plan to hand southern pilot zones to the Lebanese army. The five-party de-confliction cell has not convened and the hand-off has not begun.

The disputed killing of Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah hardened rather than resolved: Israel published a military ID and footage it says show he was a Hamas operative, while Al Jazeera calls it the deliberate targeting of a journalist, the network's twelfth media worker killed.

Sakana's Fugu Ultra results remain developer-led rather than independently evaluated, and Anthropic has not named the “Qwable” distillation, leaving the frontier claims unverified even as the agent-tooling boom and its security problems accelerate.

China defended fresh patrols in the waters east of Taiwan after Britain, France and Germany flagged the Pacific-side activity as a risk to regional stability, with Taipei's ministry warning that its warning time keeps shrinking.

Kyiv's long-range strikes hit a major Russian natural-gas plant and satellite-linked sites, extending the deep-strike campaign to the energy and supply infrastructure behind the front while seaborne crude keeps flowing.
